text: The surgeon uses indocyanine green to illuminate the infundibulum of the gallbladder, isolating the cystic duct and artery to achieve the critical view of safety.

The surgeon isolates the cystic artery, achieving the critical view of safety with both the cystic duct and artery clearly visible. The triangle of Calot is exposed, with Ruvier's sulcus visible in the background.

The surgeon approaches the cystic plate, with Ruvier's sulcus visible to the left. The cystic plate appears narrow initially. The surgeon uses a cautery device, applying a double or triple pump technique to achieve thermal spread on the vessels.

The surgeon carefully preserves the posterior artery traveling up the cystic plate, using bipolar cautery for control. Occasional back bleeding is observed from the gallbladder, with no bleeding from the liver or cystic plate side.
